stories.headline: BTC slides to $76.3K as UMich sentiment hits ATL 49.8, $281M longs liquidated pre-FOMC
stories.tldr: Bitcoin failed at $80K and bled to $76,342 Tuesday as consumer sentiment crashed to a record low and traders unwound risk hours before the Fed.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Macro is now overriding crypto-native flows — a dovish Powell tomorrow is the only thing standing between BTC and a retest of the $65K March lows.
the_details: BTC opened at $77,368 and printed an intraday low of $76,342.57 by 9 a.m. ET; long liquidations topped $281M.
University of Michigan consumer sentiment fell to 49.8, an all-time low, with 1-year inflation expectations jumping to 4.8% from 3.8%.
Total crypto market cap dropped 2.53% to $2.54T as the Fear & Greed Index slid to 39 — back in fear territory.

stories.headline: DeFi United unveils rescue plan for $292M Kelp DAO exploit with 107K rsETH stuck in Aave
stories.tldr: A coalition of crypto projects released a technical proposal Tuesday to restore rsETH backing after the Lazarus-linked Kelp DAO bridge drain.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Roughly 107,000 unbacked rsETH are still collateralizing positions in Aave and Compound — a coordinated unwind is the only way to avoid cascading liquidations across DeFi blue chips.
the_details: Attacker forged a bridge message that minted 116,500 rsETH on Ethereum without backing on April 18, draining ~$292M.
Coalition spans multiple blockchain teams aiming to socialize losses and reseed reserves rather than freeze contracts.
Drift Protocol's earlier $285M Solana drain on April 1 — also tied to North Korea's Lazarus Group — pushed April hack losses past $606M.

stories.headline: 100+ crypto firms led by Coinbase, Ripple press Senate to mark up Clarity Act now
stories.tldr: A coalition of more than 100 crypto firms is pushing the Senate Banking Committee to advance the Clarity Act and codify the SEC-CFTC division of crypto oversight.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Without legislation, the March SEC-CFTC joint interpretive release is just guidance — a future administration could rip it up overnight, leaving builders back in regulatory limbo.
the_details: March 17 SEC-CFTC joint release laid out a 5-category token taxonomy across 68 pages, superseding the 2019 framework.
Coalition demands include developer protections, easier disclosure rules, and locked-in stablecoin reward preservation.
April 13 SEC statement already cleared 'Covered User Interface Providers' to operate without broker-dealer registration.

stories.headline: Injective v1.19.0 mainnet upgrade goes live with new INJ buyback mechanism
stories.tldr: Injective shipped its v1.19.0 mainnet upgrade at 14:30 UTC Tuesday, activating a new token buyback engine that reroutes protocol revenue into INJ.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Hardcoded buybacks turn Injective's exchange fees into a structural sink for INJ supply — an aggressive flywheel pitch as L1 token economics come back into vogue.
the_details: Bybit, Binance and other CEXs paused INJ deposits and withdrawals from 13:30 UTC for the upgrade window.
New buyback module is positioned as a competitive answer to Hyperliquid's revenue-driven HYPE bid.
Upgrade lands as Bittensor (TAO) and other AI-adjacent alts log +47% YTD even with BTC dominance pinned at 60%.

stories.headline: TOKEN2049 Dubai pushed to 2027 as organizers cite heightened UAE security risks
stories.tldr: TOKEN2049's Dubai stop has been moved to 2027, with organizers pointing to elevated regional security risks tied to the Iran conflict.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Losing the Middle East's flagship crypto event for a year strips a major deal-flow venue and signals that geopolitics is now bleeding into the conference and capital-formation calendar.
the_details: Organizers cited heightened security concerns in the UAE, the same Iran-tension backdrop driving the latest UMich inflation spike.
Singapore stop remains the only confirmed 2026 TOKEN2049 — concentrating Asian crypto activity into a single venue.
Move follows a wave of relocated industry events as funds rebalance travel and side-event spend toward Asia and the U.S.
